description: The recorded known-good B2 job oracle carries no structural-trust flags.
A degenerate assert-True oracle is flagged (an assertion that can never fail grades nothing).
A module-level pytest.skip is flagged (the oracle never grades anything).
oracle_inline:
import pytest
pytest.skip('graded elsewhere', allow_module_level=True)

def test_real():
    assert 1 + 1 == 2

description: An oracle with no test functions is flagged.
Unparseable oracle source is a measured finding, never a harness crash.
SOUNDNESS: the known-good oracle must PASS the frozen known-good B2 implementation (a failure = an oracle that punishes correct code).
SENSITIVITY: breaking word_frequencies (returns {}) must make the oracle FAIL (a pass = an oracle blind to the break).
SENSITIVITY: breaking neighbor_pairs (returns {}) must make the oracle FAIL.
FAILURE-MODE VOCABULARY: an oracle importing a module that does not exist must classify as collection-error (the #752-F3 plumbing class), never as a plain test failure.
MODEL-IN-THE-LOOP: a freshly 14B-generated job oracle for the B2 gold goal must pass the frozen known-good implementation (live soundness; wired at the first live slot, #765).
A test function with zero assertions is flagged low-assertions.
A minimal healthy inline oracle (real import, real assertion) carries no flags.
A mixed oracle (one real test + one assert-True padding test) is flagged degenerate.
KNOWN LIMITATION PINNED: assertions living only in a helper the test calls read as low-assertions (the ast scan counts asserts inside the test function; helper-indirection is not followed).
A multi-test healthy oracle carries no flags.
SOUNDNESS: a minimal correct inline oracle (tokenize subset) passes the known-good reference.
SENSITIVITY: breaking tokenize (returns []) must make the oracle FAIL.
SENSITIVITY: breaking combined_report (returns an empty string) must make the oracle FAIL.

DEFENSE-IN-DEPTH: an all-module-skip oracle collects ZERO tests, so the runner classifies it collection-error (pytest exit 5) — it can never read as a pass. The static module-skip flag is the second, source-level layer.
oracle_inline:
import pytest
pytest.skip('graded elsewhere', allow_module_level=True)

def test_never_runs():
    assert False

description: FAILURE-MODE VOCABULARY: syntactically broken oracle source classifies as collection-error at execution.
FAILURE-MODE VOCABULARY: importing a missing NAME from an existing module classifies as collection-error (ImportError at collection), never a plain failure.
CONTRACT: the known-good B2 job oracle imports only symbols the (complete) declared task contracts export.
CONTRACT (the literal #752-F3 example): an oracle importing analyze_text from text_analyzer, a symbol no declared task contract exports, is flagged - the coder was never told to build it.
CONTRACT: the SAME known-good B2 oracle is flagged when the declared task contracts omit one real export (combined_report) - an under-declared plan, not a bad oracle, still surfaces as a defect here.

the tool counts how often each word appears

criteria.text: the tool counts adjacent word pairs

description: CRITERIA-COVERAGE: all four real B2 behavior criteria lexically match a test in the known-good job oracle.

criteria.check: running with --version exits printing a semantic version string
criteria.text: the tool prints its version number when given a --version flag

description: CRITERIA-COVERAGE: a fifth criterion (a --version flag) the known-good oracle never tests is flagged uncovered - a real coverage gap in an otherwise-sound oracle.

criteria.text: the widget merges two quantities into a running total

description: KNOWN LIMITATION PINNED: a test that correctly covers a criterion but is phrased with disjoint vocabulary (a genuine paraphrase) reads as uncovered - this is exact-token lexical overlap, not semantic understanding (see criteria_coverage_findings).

criteria.check: looks like the client's brand colors
criteria.text: the app looks professional and modern

description: CRITERIA-COVERAGE: a visual-tier criterion is outside behavior/smoke and is never counted covered or uncovered - zero test-tier criteria supplied yields a vacuously clean flags_empty verdict.
